title: 5) Needling techniques
text:
①Palpate the temporalis with bite and release to ﬁnd the part that is thicker when biting.
Insert the needle on that point toward the top of the head. [0.12mm/ transverse/
5~10mm]
②Palpate the temporalis on the line of eyes, insert the needle parallel toward the ear.
[0.12mm/ transverse/ 5~10mm]
③Above the middle of apex of ear (Auricularis Superior), insert the needle toward the
back side of the head. [0.12mm/ transverse/ 5~10mm]
~ !  ~
97
Facial  Cosmetic Acupuncture
④ST-7, there are two common methods. I introduced the ﬁrst one in this book. First, let
patient’s mouth slightly open and relaxed. If it opens wide, ST-7 is blocked with the
mandible bone.
(a) As a traditional point, insert a needle in perpendicular or slightly toward the cheek.
[0.16mm/ perpendicular or slightly oblique/ 10-15mm]
(b)As a muscular approach, for stimulation on the lateral pterygoid, insert the needle
around 30-40mm toward the zygomatic bone. You can feel more dense texture at
the tip of the needle. [0.20mm/ perpendicular-oblique/ 30-40mm]
⑤ST-6, regular needling on the masseter. [0.20mm/ perpendicular/ 10~15mm]
⑥Around ST-6, target is the tightened point on the masseter. Palpate ﬁrst and release the
muscle with acupuncture. [0.20mm/ perpendicular/ 10~15mm] Gentle rubbing after
acupuncture is recommended. I inserted only one needle in this book, but you can
insert more needles if you ﬁnd more points.
~ !  ~
98
Facial  Cosmetic Acupuncture

* Fill in the blanks by choosing answers from the box.
1.
You need to be very careful with needle insertion in this area, the thinnest and
sensitive skin around the _________.
2.
Levator labii superioris alaeque nasi, levator labii superioris, and _________ are
related to the nasolabial folds.
3.
The  _________ of the lip is more sensitive, so we should be more careful for needling.
4.
In the forehead line treatment, _________ point is sensitive to pain. When you insert a
needle on this point, you should hold the skin with the other hand to keep it from
moving.
5.
If the practitioner inserts needles on the tightened masseter, usually _________ angle
insertion is recommended.
Answers: 1.(10)   2.(7)   3.(8)   4.(2)   5.(4)
~ !
~
101
(1) Nose                                                  (6) Depressor Labii Inferioris
(2)  GB-14                                               (7) Zygomaticus Major/Minor
(3)  DU-24                                              (8) Upper area
(4)  Perpendicular (90°)                        (9) Lower area
(5)  Transverse (15°)                              (10) Eyes
REVIEW
